```
    35
```

```
     1	import type { FC, ReactNode } from 'react'
     2	
     3	import type { DialogFlipDirection } from './index'
     4	
     5	export interface DialogStackItemProps {
     6	  title: ReactNode
     7	  description?: ReactNode
     8	  content: FC<{ dismiss: () => void }>
     9	  className?: string
    10	  from?: DialogFlipDirection
    11	  showCloseButton?: boolean
    12	  clickOutsideToDismiss?: boolean
    13	}
    14	
    15	export interface DialogStackItem extends DialogStackItemProps {
    16	  id: string
    17	}
    18	
    19	let stack: DialogStackItem[] = []
    20	let idCounter = 0
    21	const listeners = new Set<() => void>()
    22	
    23	function emit() {
    24	  for (const fn of listeners) fn()
    25	}
    26	
    27	export function subscribe(fn: () => void) {
    28	  listeners.add(fn)
    29	  return () => {
    30	    listeners.delete(fn)
    31	  }
    32	}
    33	
    34	export function getSnapshot(): DialogStackItem[] {
    35	  return stack
    36	}
    37	
    38	export function presentDialog(props: DialogStackItemProps): () => void {
    39	  const id = `dialog-${++idCounter}`
    40	  stack = [...stack, { ...props, id }]
    41	  emit()
    42	  return () => dismissDialog(id)
    43	}
    44	
    45	export function dismissDialog(id: string) {
    46	  stack = stack.filter((item) => item.id !== id)
    47	  emit()
    48	}
    49	
    50	export function dismissTopDialog() {
    51	  stack = stack.slice(0, -1)
    52	  emit()
    53	}
    54	
    55	export function dismissAllDialogs() {
    56	  stack = []
    57	  emit()
    58	}
```

```
    53	  constructor(alertType: AlertType, key?: NodeKey) {
    54	    super(key)
    55	    this.__alertType = alertType
    56	  }
    57	
    58	  createDOM(_config: EditorConfig): HTMLElement {
    59	    const blockquote = document.createElement('blockquote')
    60	    blockquote.className = `rich-alert rich-alert-${this.__alertType}`
    61	    blockquote.dataset.alertType = this.__alertType
    62	    blockquote.dataset.alertNodeKey = this.__key
    63	    blockquote.prepend(createAlertHeader(this.__alertType))
    64	    return blockquote
    65	  }
    66	
    67	  updateDOM(prevNode: AlertQuoteNode, dom: HTMLElement): boolean {
    68	    if (prevNode.__alertType !== this.__alertType) {
    69	      dom.className = `rich-alert rich-alert-${this.__alertType}`
    70	      dom.dataset.alertType = this.__alertType
    71	      const header = dom.querySelector('.rich-alert-header')
    72	      if (header) {
    73	        header.className = `rich-alert-header rich-alert-header-${this.__alertType}`
    74	      }
    75	    }
    76	    return false
    77	  }
    78	
    79	  static importJSON(serializedNode: SerializedAlertQuoteNode): AlertQuoteNode {
    80	    return $createAlertQuoteNode(serializedNode.alertType)
    81	  }
    82	
    83	  exportJSON(): SerializedAlertQuoteNode {
    84	    return {
    85	      ...super.exportJSON(),
    86	      type: 'alert-quote',
    87	      alertType: this.__alertType,
    88	      version: 1,
    89	    }
    90	  }
    91	
    92	  getAlertType(): AlertType {
    93	    return this.__alertType
    94	  }
    95	
    96	  setAlertType(alertType: AlertType): void {
    97	    const writable = this.getWritable()
    98	    writable.__alertType = alertType
    99	  }
   100	
   101	  getDOMSlot(element: HTMLElement) {
   102	    const header = element.querySelector('.rich-alert-header')
   103	    return super.getDOMSlot(element).withAfter(header)
   104	  }
   105	
   106	  isInline(): boolean {
   107	    return false
   108	  }
   109	}
   110	
   111	export function $createAlertQuoteNode(alertType: AlertType): AlertQuoteNode {
   112	  return new AlertQuoteNode(alertType)
   113	}
```

```
    19	  important: MessageSquareWarning,
    20	  warning: TriangleAlert,
    21	  caution: OctagonAlert,
    22	}
    23	
    24	const ALERT_LABELS: Record<AlertType, string> = {
    25	  note: 'Note',
    26	  tip: 'Tip',
    27	  important: 'Important',
    28	  warning: 'Warning',
    29	  caution: 'Caution',
    30	}
    31	
    32	const ALERT_COLORS: Record<AlertType, string> = {
    33	  note: '#3b82f6',
    34	  tip: '#22c55e',
    35	  important: '#a855f7',
    36	  warning: '#f59e0b',
    37	  caution: '#ef4444',
    38	}
    39	
    40	const ALL_TYPES: AlertType[] = ['note', 'tip', 'important', 'warning', 'caution']
    41	
    42	export const AlertRenderer: FC<AlertRendererProps> = ({
    43	  type,
    44	  editable,
    45	  onTypeChange,
    46	}) => {
    47	  const Icon = ALERT_ICONS[type]
    48	  const label = ALERT_LABELS[type]
    49	
    50	  if (!editable || !onTypeChange) {
    51	    return (
    52	      <div className="rr-alert-header" data-alert-type={type}>
    53	        <Icon className="rr-alert-icon" />
    54	        <span className="rr-alert-label">{label}</span>
    55	      </div>
    56	    )
    57	  }
    58	
    59	  return (
    60	    <div className="rr-alert-header" data-alert-type={type}>
    61	      <DropdownMenu>
    62	        <DropdownMenuTrigger className="rr-alert-trigger">
    63	          <Icon className="rr-alert-icon" />
    64	          <span className="rr-alert-label">{label}</span>
    65	          <ChevronDown className="rr-alert-chevron" />
    66	        </DropdownMenuTrigger>
    67	        <DropdownMenuContent sideOffset={6} align="start">
    68	          {ALL_TYPES.map((t) => {
    69	            const ItemIcon = ALERT_ICONS[t]
    70	            return (
    71	              <DropdownMenuItem
    72	                key={t}
    73	                data-alert-type={t}
    74	                onClick={() => onTypeChange(t)}
    75	              >
    76	                <ItemIcon
    77	                  className="rr-alert-menu-icon"
    78	                  style={{ color: ALERT_COLORS[t] }}
    79	                />
    80	                <span>{ALERT_LABELS[t]}</span>
    81	              </DropdownMenuItem>
    82	            )
    83	          })}
    84	        </DropdownMenuContent>
    85	      </DropdownMenu>
    86	    </div>
    87	  )
    88	}
    89	
    90	export default AlertRenderer
```
</details></tool-use>

```
+    headerEl,
+  )
+}

+          return changed ? next : prev
+        })
+      })
+    })
+  }, [editor])
```

```
     4	export function getStrictContext<T>(
     5	  name?: string,
     6	): readonly [
     7	  (props: { value: T; children?: ReactNode }) => ReactNode,
     8	  () => T,
     9	] {
    10	  const Context = createContext<T | undefined>(undefined)
    11	
    12	  const Provider = ({
    13	    value,
    14	    children,
    15	  }: {
    16	    value: T
    17	    children?: ReactNode
    18	  }) => <Context.Provider value={value}>{children}</Context.Provider>
    19

    21	    const ctx = useContext(Context)
    22	    if (ctx === undefined) {
    23	      throw new Error(`useContext must be used within ${name ?? 'a Provider'}`)
    24	    }
    25	    return ctx
    26	  }
    27	
    28	  return [Provider, useSafeContext] as const
```

_**Agent (gpt-5.3-codex 2026-02-11 14:10:23Z)**_

1. [HIGH] Alert Headers Regress To Empty In Read-Only Rendering  
File: `packages/rich-editor/src/nodes/AlertQuoteNode.ts:28`  
Issue: `createAlertHeader` now creates an empty `.rich-alert-header` (icon/label removed). `RichRenderer` does not mount `AlertPlugin`, so read-only content can render alert blocks with blank headers.  
Fix: Keep a fallback label/icon in node DOM, then let plugin/UI override when available; or mount equivalent alert-header rendering in `RichRenderer`.

2. [HIGH] Unvalidated Video URL Used In Download Flow  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-video/src/VideoRenderer.tsx:159`  
Issue: `src` is directly used in `fetch(src)` and fallback anchor `href`. If `src` is user-controlled, unsafe/non-http schemes can be triggered.  
Fix: Parse and allowlist protocols (`http:`, `https:`; optionally `blob:`), and block/disable download for anything else.

3. [MEDIUM] Wildcard Dependency Introduces Supply-Chain Drift  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-codeblock/package.json:24`  
Issue: `"shikicode": "*"` is not pinned, so installs can silently change behavior/security posture over time.  
Fix: Pin to a bounded semver range (or exact version) and update via controlled dependency tooling.

4. [MEDIUM] Alert Plugin Uses Private Lexical API + Full-Map Scans  
File: `packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/AlertPlugin.tsx:92`  
Issue: Reads `editor.getEditorState()._nodeMap` (private internal API) and performs broad scans/sync passes, which is brittle and can hurt performance on large documents.  
Fix: Use public Lexical APIs and mutation payloads to update only affected alert nodes.

5. [MEDIUM] Mermaid SVG Encoding Can Fail On Large Payloads  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-mermaid/src/MermaidRenderer.tsx:136`  
Issue: `String.fromCharCode(...Uint8Array)` spreads the whole buffer into function args; large diagrams can throw `RangeError`/freeze UI.  
Fix: Use `Blob` + `URL.createObjectURL` (with cleanup) or chunked encoding.

6. [MEDIUM] Missing Error Handling For Mermaid Dynamic Imports  
File: `packages/rich-renderer-mermaid/src/MermaidRenderer.tsx:63`  
Issue: Dynamic imports are `.then(...)` without `.catch(...)`; import failures can leave loading state unresolved and create unhandled promise rejections.  
Fix: Add catch paths that set error state and clear loading safely.

7. [LOW] `clickOutsideToDismiss` API Is Declared But Unused  
File: `packages/rich-editor-ui/src/components/dialog/store.ts:12`  
Issue: Prop exists in public type but is never consumed in stack rendering logic, so callers cannot rely on it.  
Fix: Wire it into close behavior (backdrop/escape) or remove the option.

8. [LOW] Missing Tests For New Critical Behaviors  
File: `packages/rich-editor/src/plugins/AlertPlugin.tsx:65`  
Issue: Major new behavior (alert header portal sync, mermaid editing, video download path, code editor integration) landed without corresponding tests in this diff.  
Fix: Add focused integration/unit tests for these flows and regressions.

Open questions/assumptions:
1. Assumed `src`/diagram/link payloads can come from untrusted document content; if strictly trusted, security severity for URL issues can be reduced.
2. Build check passed for affected packages, but no new automated test coverage was included.

Verdict: BLOCK
